title: Achasma walang
intro: Achasma walang, also known as "Malabar nut" or "Indian brain", is a plant species in the family Achariaceae, traditionally used for its medicinal and cognitive-enhancing properties. The plant has been reported to possess health benefits, including its ability to improve memory, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of Achasma walang include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Achasma walang is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, often combined with other herbs to enhance its cognitive-enhancing and adaptogenic properties.
taxonomy: Achasma walang is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asparagaceae. It is native to the Philippines and consists of at least two species, including Achasma walang and another species that has not been widely classified. These plants are known for their unique appearance and have been studied for their potential medicinal properties. The classification of Achasma walang is still a subject of ongoing research and may be revised as new information becomes available.
